channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LCS Spring 2026 Records Record Low Viewership
item.content:encoded:
The LCS Spring 2026 was the least-watched regular season in North American League of Legends history.

Rebranding back to the LCS from the LTA initially led to positive change, with the LCS 2026 Lock-In seeing a slight increase in peak viewership from the previous year’s iteration.

However, according to Esports Charts, Spring 2026 earned 3.33 million hours watched across seven weeks, marking a 15% drop from the LTA North 2025 Split 2.

Meanwhile, the final week clash between Team Liquid and Cloud9 attracted Spring’s peak audience of just 86.6k.

LCS Spring 2026 also saw a decline in its own viewership as it progressed, with almost a quarter of the average viewers dropping out.

While local interest is waning, the LCS seems to be mostly struggling for international viewership. Portuguese-speaking viewership, mostly from Brazil, dropped by 75% compared to the LTA North last year.

The peak for the league came nine years ago, as the LCS Spring 2017 peaked at almost 650k viewers.

There’s Still Hope for the LCS

Despite the regular season struggles, the LCS Spring Playoffs have performed relatively well so far.

The Playoffs have not yet concluded, but have already seen a significant 30% increase in peak viewership from the LTA North Split 2 2025 Playoffs.

The five-game series between Cloud9 and FlyQuest in the opening round saw a peak audience of 158k.

And that number could inflate significantly with popular streamer Nicholas ‘Jynxzi’ Stewart revealing he wants to co-stream the LCS finals.

Over 900k viewers watched Jynxi’s content creator LoL tournament, making it the fourth most-watched LoL event in 2026.

What do you think Riot can do to return people’s interest in the LCS? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Popular streamer and Shopify Rebellion co-owner Charles Christopher ‘MoistCr1TiKaL’ White Jr. says the LCS needs more content creators.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Rayman Legends Remake Dubbed ‘Retold’ Leaks Online
item.content:encoded:
We reported a couple of years ago that a Rayman Legends Remake was in the works at Ubisoft, and it’s one of the projects that’s managed to dodge recent cancelations. A Rayman Legends: Retold logo has appeared online, and we can happily reveal this is real.

The development of the Rayman Legends Remake was bolstered by the former Prince of Persia team. The team has been hard at work on a reimaging of the beloved platformer, and the good news was added to a few months later by our exclusive report about Ubisoft reaffirming that it’s working on ‘The Future of Rayman.’

That future is nearly here.

Insider Gaming understands that the reveal of Rayman Legends: Retold, AKA Project Steambot, is expected within the next week and that the game is scheduled to release on October 1, 2026.

It’s the perfect gift for Rayman and platforming fans with Summer Game Fest 2026 season around the corner!

The Rayman Legends Retold logo suddenly appeared and began circulating on social media on May 27. Obviously, when something like this appears out of the blue without context, there’s an element of immediate skepticism.

As I said, Insider Gaming has been well aware of the project for a long time, and this is just the inevitable confirmation of it.

There was a fear that it would succumb to the wave of cancelations that took place at the start of 2026, an unfortunate day that included the Prince of Persia: The Sands of Time Remake being canceled.

Stay tuned for a full reveal of the Rayman Legends Remake, now known as ‘Rayman Legends Retold.’

Are you hyped for a Rayman Legends Remake? Is it an immediate day-one buy? Let us know via the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more on IG, make sure you don’t miss out on our debut Insider Gaming Showcase on May 28!

For even more news, read about Destiny 2 players aiming to ‘Slam Servers’ with big June 9 party and upcoming Game Pass titles surfacing ahead of the Xbox Showcase.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
